title: Reserve capacity for canary SLO under production saturation
description: Gate 4 canary can fail honestly when all production ez-runner-c runners are busy with worldarchitect.ai backlog. Add a canary-only runner pool or equivalent capacity reservation so the SLO measures infrastructure health without competing with arbitrary production backlog.
notes: 2026-07-07 Codex live proof: repo-scoped canary daemon active from /home/jleechan/.config/ezgha/canary.toml; Docker container ez-canary-runner-b-1 running; repo runner API sees ez-canary-runner-b-1 online and org runner API sees 0 matching canary runners. Gate 4 fresh canary run https://github.com/jleechanorg/ez-gh-actions/actions/runs/28886545592 completed success on ez-canary-runner-b-1, queued 2026-07-07T17:41:43Z, started 2026-07-07T17:41:47Z, time_to_start=4s under 90s SLO. Full CANARY_CONFIG_FILE verifier passed Gates 0,1,2,3,4,7,10. Curie adversarial blocker fixed before push: stop_all now filters containers by configured prefix so production stop does not kill canary containers, and Gate 3 reads top-level state_dir plus filters Docker count by prefix so mixed canary/prod containers cannot mask missing capacity.
close_reason: done

title: hostname-scoped deregistration residual collision + 'host' fallback
description: our_runner_prefix() (docker_backend.rs:143-145) scopes stop-deregistration by hostname, but hostnames are not unique across fleets (cloned VMs, 'ubuntu', Mac.local). Worse: hostname() (docker_backend.rs:24-31) falls back to literal 'host' on failure, so two degraded hosts both use ezgha-host- and can deregister each other's runners. Residual of the fixed fleet-wide-stop critical. Fix: include machine-id/uuid persisted in config at init. MISSED by workflow review fix-verification (Workflow 3).

title: chore(watchdog): commit or delete external ~/.local/bin/ezgha-fleet-watchdog.sh
description: Review 20260706 PARTIAL: the only automatic capacity-shortfall detector is an uncommitted out-of-repo script with a false header ('serve does not top-up'), ssh-timeout breakage, restart-only remediation — violates Gate 7 committed-config rule and CLAUDE.md automation-completeness. Either install via install-service and version it, or delete once in-daemon alerting lands.
notes: 2026-07-07 /nextsteps: external watchdog was patched out-of-repo to run locally on Linux instead of SSHing to jeff-ubuntu, and to avoid false restarts when container count dips but slot_assignments has all configured slots reserved. Still violates committed-config rule until versioned/installed or removed.

title: fix(src/main.rs): wait_for_backend in-process retry around choose_backend
description: Currently src/main.rs:201-220 (Commands::Serve arm) calls choose_backend(cfg)?; — if it returns Err (e.g. Selection::None because lima isn't up yet), main() exits code 1, systemd restarts at RestartSec=10. The Jul 6 incident produced 1381 of these. Replace the immediate ? with wait_for_backend(cfg)?; which loops with capped exponential backoff (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 300s-cap) and only returns Ok once choose_backend succeeds. Once the daemon is in its main 30s loop, transient docker-socket blips are already contained; this change specifically protects the cold-start path. Concrete Rust patch in advisor review Q7. Verify with cargo test, then rebuild + restart per Gate 0.
notes: Implemented a9a7ab1: wait_for_backend() retries backend::select() for up to 120s, sleeping 5s between attempts. serve subcommand uses this instead of choose_backend().

title: list_runners silently truncates at 100 (no pagination)
description: github.rs:74 uses per_page=100 with no pagination. Org/repo with >100 runners: stop never deregisters runners past page 1; status undercounts. Silent truncation. Fix: paginate via gh api --paginate with a slurp-compatible parse, or loop pages until short page.
close_reason: Stale in Rust: list_runners uses --paginate --slurp with explanatory comment (github.rs:226-236). Remaining copy of the bug is Gate 3 in verify-exit-criteria.sh — tracked under k4h.

title: fix(lima-vm@colima.service): Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes + drop --foreground + TimeoutSec=300
description: Current unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 and runs 'limactl start colima --foreground' in the foreground. On warm boot (VM already running) this completes in 16ms and seems fine; on cold boot, limactl needs minutes to bring up the QEMU guest, gets SIGKILLed by TimeoutSec=10, and the unit fails. Verified empirically with 'limactl start colima' on running VM (16ms 'Using the existing instance') and watching the cold-boot get killed. Fix unit to: drop --foreground (limactl daemonizes itself, returns when VM is SSH-able), Type=oneshot + RemainAfterExit=yes (so unit is 'active' after limactl returns, until asked to stop), TimeoutSec=10 → 300 (bound worst-case cold-boot cloud-init). Type=notify is NOT viable because limactl doesn't emit sd_notify. This single change is the highest leverage in the fix set; without it, no consumer-side Requires= will work on cold-boot.
notes: Implemented f202638: lima-vm@colima.service patched to Type=forking, drop --foreground, RemainAfterExit=yes, TimeoutStartSec=300, Restart=no, ExecStop=. Deployed + checked in to docs/lima-vm-colima.service.

title: Diagnose Gate 4 canary SLO failure for run 28872957247
Add queue backlog drain and superseded-run cleanup policy
description: 2026-07-07 investigation found worldarchitect.ai saturation, not label mismatch: ~90+ queued runs, 20 in_progress, oldest fresh queued >200m, all ez-runner-c runners online/busy. Add an explicit queue-drain policy/tooling that identifies obsolete or superseded queued workflow runs before cancellation, reports exact run URLs, and avoids destructive broad cancellation without evidence.
notes: CLOSED 2026-07-07 Codex: implemented dry-run-first backlog drain tooling. scripts/queue-backlog-drain.sh lists queued tail and superseded candidates with exact URLs; scripts/cleanup-stuck-runs.sh now defaults to dry-run, adds --superseded, requires --apply for mutation, avoids in-progress runs, and keeps broad tail cancellation explicit. Tests: docs/test-queue-backlog-drain.sh and docs/test-cleanup-stuck-runs.sh.

title: fix(main.rs): single-instance flock/PID lock to prevent concurrent serve processes
description: Two ezgha serve processes against same config path = TOCTOU on slot file: both read assignments, both call next_slot(), allocate same slot, collide on docker run --name. Write PID lock file in config dir. Guard in serve subcommand. On lock fail: bail with helpful error.
close_reason: Shipped 9e37677: flock serve.lock present on Mac+Linux (~/.config/ezgha/serve.lock)

title: incident: 1381-restart storm 2026-07-06 — root cause lima VM mis-target + bailing on absent backend
description: On 2026-07-06 09:02:57–12:59:06 PDT, ezgha.service entered a 3h55m loop of exit-1 systemd restarts because (a) the user's docker CLI is pinned to lima-colima context but ezgha.service only declares After=docker.service, and (b) lima-vm@colima.service has Type=simple + TimeoutSec=10 which kills limactl start before the QEMU guest boots. ezgha.service then bails on Selection::None (src/main.rs:93) and is hammered by systemd RestartSec=10. Service self-healed in 2 minutes once operator manually ran 'limactl start colima' at 12:59:06. Forensic timeline + git/diff details in PR description. Root-cause-by: read-only investigation (this bead exists as the parent for the fix set; sub-beads link back here).
notes: Root cause fixed: lima VM no autostart + wrong After=docker.service + no StartLimitBurst. Fixed d55f191. limactl start-at-login colima registered.

title: Design periodic GitHub Actions stuck-run reaper
notes: Design target: add a periodic, dry-run-first stuck-run reaper for jleechanorg/worldarchitect.ai and other QUEUE_REPO values. It must be job-aware: do not classify a run as stale only by workflow_run.created_at because reruns/current attempts can have old run.created_at but new job.started_at/run_started_at. Proposed policy: every 10-15 minutes, scan queued and in_progress runs with REST; fetch jobs for candidates; compute current_attempt_age from max(job.started_at, run.run_started_at, run.updated_at) for queued jobs. Actions: (1) cancel-then-delete only queued run objects whose run.created_at > 8h AND current_attempt_age > 60-90m, (2) cancel superseded queued runs only when a newer queued or in_progress run exists for same repo/workflow/branch and candidate current_attempt_age > 20-30m, (3) never touch in_progress by default; report in_progress jobs over policy separately with runner/job URL, (4) label-mismatch detector reports queued jobs whose labels have no matching online runner labels, e.g. agent-orchestrator or warp-*; it does not cancel them. Install path: systemd user timer on Linux host using ~/.cargo/bin/ezgha or scripts/reap-stuck-runs.sh, with log file under ~/.local/state/ezgha/reaper.jsonl and alert() on mutations/failures. Safety: dry-run default, --apply only for timer after config enables it, max mutations per run, REST rate-limit floor, lock file, exact URLs in logs, no tail cancellation without explicit allowlist.

title: Verify Mac ezgha installed and running latest

title: fix(src/main.rs Init): refuse runner.count above daemon capacity
description: Companion to ez-gh-actions-vmz (effective_limits over-commit). After fixing effective_limits() in src/docker_backend.rs:241-254 to clamp per-runner share to capacity/count, update src/main.rs:120-124 Init arm to refuse runner.count > max(1, ncpu / cfg.limits.cpus.max(0.5)) rather than refusing only on memory. Today a user can set count=16 on a 4-CPU daemon with no per-CPU check. After patch: 'error: cfg.runner.count=16 exceeds capacity max(1, 4 cpus / 0.5 cpus/runner) = 8 runners; lower cfg.runner.count, raise cfg.limits.cpus (but not above ncpu), or provision more CPUs.' Modifies only the Init arm of src/main.rs; cargo test + Gate 0-10 re-verify.
close_reason: Stale/done: init capacity bail and aggregate clamp are implemented in src/main.rs and src/docker_backend.rs; roadmap already treats this as closed.

Cuts future mean-time-to-diagnose from minutes to seconds. Patch is ~15 lines in src/main.rs:84-94. Concrete patch in advisor review Q8.

title: fix(ezgha.service): per-host drop-in to require lima-vm@colima.service
description: Base unit at ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service (generated by src/service.rs:install_systemd) currently has 'After=network-online.target docker.service' which targets the wrong daemon. Create a per-host drop-in at ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service.d/10-lima.conf with 'Requires=lima-vm@colima.service' to make the dependency real on this colima-based host. Drop-in (not base-unit edit) keeps the install portable for Mac/Docker Desktop and Linux/system-docker hosts where lima-vm@colima.service does not exist (Requires= on a missing unit makes 'systemctl --user enable' fail with 'Unit lima-vm@colima.service is masked or not found'). Follows the existing convention set by hermes-gateway.service.d/10-env-file.conf on this same box.
notes: Implemented in d55f191: service.rs now emits After=lima-vm@colima.service + Wants= in install_systemd(). Deployed service unit updated. limactl start-at-login run.

notes: Implemented gh API backoff + rate-limit classification in github.rs with exponential retry and bounded jitterless delays; added unit tests for rate-limit parsing and backoff classification.

title: Pace runner respawns to avoid tripping host watchdog(8) reboot
description: ensure_count/docker_backend currently can respawn many runner slots simultaneously (JIT + docker run per slot), which spikes 1-min load average enough to trip /etc/watchdog.conf's max-load-1=24 threshold on this 32-thread box -- confirmed root cause of two host reboots today (00:48 loadavg 71 on a full cold respawn of 16; 12:50 loadavg 25-28), the second of which killed the runner-truly-healthy mission's predecessor sidekick session outright. Operator/session-level discipline (checking uptime before every systemctl restart) is not durable -- it dies with the session. Fix: add bounded concurrency to ensure_count (e.g. max 3-4 simultaneous docker run + JIT registration calls in flight, batched with a short sleep between batches) and/or a load-aware backoff (check /proc/loadavg or getloadavg() before spawning a batch, delay if already elevated) in docker_backend.rs, so the daemon is safe to restart by ANY operator at ANY time regardless of how many slots need respawning at once. This also directly serves the E1/INV-1 utilization invariant: gentler respawn waves mean shallower busy-count dips, which should show up as fewer inv1_fail_class=offline-respawning samples in invariant_history.jsonl once this lands -- that data itself is a validation signal for the fix. Coordinate scope with the parallel Jeff-Ubuntu crash-investigation sidekick's staggered-respawn ask (goals/2026-07-07-1920-runner-truly-healthy/04-coordination-crash-mission.md) to avoid duplicating work. Also feeds E4's ed8 fleet-churn root-cause requirement (docs/ed8-fleet-churn-root-cause-20260707.md).

Looks correct and well-matched to the brief. HOLDING merge/deploy per main's instruction -- user invoked ultracode, main running adversarial review of this diff since it touches the exact respawn path that caused today's watchdog reboots. Awaiting verdict.

title: disk floor guard fails open when measurement fails
description: docker_backend.rs:205-207 free_disk_gb()==None branch warns and SPAWNS anyway. Measurement failure correlates with the guarded condition: on a near-full daemon disk the 'docker run --rm --entrypoint df <image>' probe is what fails (ENOSPC on pull/create). Also returns None forever if image lacks df. Design philosophy is fail-closed; make None fail closed (or fall back to docker system df / docker info DockerRootDir df) and add a regression test. Found by post-hoc adversarial review; MISSED by 50-agent workflow review.
close_reason: Shipped afa8de5: 2-strike disk measurement bail in ensure_count

title: no aggregate capacity check: count x limits can exceed daemon
description: effective_limits() (docker_backend.rs:50-63) clamps a SINGLE runner to 100% of daemon capacity; ensure_count spawns runner.count of them. 4 runners x 8GB on a 16GB Colima VM are individually within limits but collectively OOM the daemon VM — the exact host-down failure mode the tool exists to prevent. Nothing divides by count except one-time init halving (which also ignores count). Fix: clamp per-runner limits to capacity/count (or check aggregate before spawn and fail closed). MISSED by workflow review.
close_reason: Shipped 077d07c: effective_limits divides daemon capacity by runner.count

title: Cap E1 invariant sampler job-enumeration cost + share fetch with queue_monitor
description: The E1 daemon-native invariant sampler (src/queue_monitor.rs, InvariantSamplerState/sample_invariants) currently calls fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet per monitored repo, which loops over EVERY queued run and calls gh api list_workflow_jobs once per run to get job-level data. With queued_jobs=1290 measured live, a single tick can take minutes of sequential API calls (observed: alert fired 263s after the sample's own ts), which likely contributes to the GitHub secondary rate limit already affecting Gate 4. Approved fix (main's design, 2026-07-07 14:xx PT), scope: (a) cap per-tick job enumeration to the OLDEST ~50 queued runs per repo -- INV-2 stays exact since the oldest job necessarily lives among the oldest runs (sort by created_at ascending, take first 50 before the per-run job fetch loop); add a queued_jobs_capped:bool field to the InvariantSample schema, true whenever a repo had more than 50 queued runs at fetch time, so the JSONL honestly signals queued_jobs is a lower bound rather than an exact count in that case. (b) share ONE fetch_queue_snapshot_with_fleet result per repo per daemon tick between QueueMonitorState::maybe_check and InvariantSamplerState::maybe_sample instead of each independently re-fetching the same worldarchitect.ai data -- likely requires restructuring main.rs's serve loop to fetch once and pass the snapshot(s) to both tick handlers, or introducing a small per-tick snapshot cache keyed by repo with a short TTL. Update/add unit tests for the cap logic (e.g. 60 queued jobs input -> only oldest 50 considered, queued_jobs_capped=true; 30 input -> all considered, queued_jobs_capped=false) and for the shared-fetch behavior if a cache is introduced. Coordinate with bead ez-gh-actions-po2 (respawn pacing) -- both target the same underlying GitHub API rate-limit pressure from different angles (this bead reduces sampler-induced API load; po2 reduces the docker-run/JIT-registration burst that trips the host watchdog) and both should be dispatched to codex together once quota returns (~14:52 PT 2026-07-07).

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: RESOLVED as an emergency fix (not deferred to codex quota return) -- this became active production harm (fleet drained to 0 containers), not speculative. Implemented fetch_capped_queue_snapshot (reverse-pagination to oldest ~50 queued runs per repo, queued_jobs_capped schema field), applied to BOTH the E1 sampler AND the pre-existing queue_monitor tick (the latter was independently causing the same starvation once the sampler was disabled as a stopgap -- confirmed via ps showing the daemon PID as parent of the in-flight gh api call). 168 tests pass, merged+deployed+verified live (samples now ~120-165s apart, fleet recovered to 16-21/22 registered). Remaining scope (sharing one snapshot per repo between the two monitors) is now a pure efficiency follow-up, not urgent -- can still go to codex at quota return.

comments.author: jleechan
comments.text: Structural time-budget fix landed (per main's directive): queue_monitor::SERVE_LOOP_TIME_BUDGET (75s), loop_start Instant threaded from main.rs's serve loop, enumerate_jobs_within_budget bails early on either monitor tick past deadline (shared budget, not 75s each). 4 new tests including the literal 'fake a slow fetch' regression test main requested (1000 synthetic items x 5ms vs 30ms budget). 171 tests pass, merged+deployed+pushed (7f7f9b7). CLAUDE.md restart-safety rule now documents the exception (draining fleet + low load = restart is the remedy). Config stopgaps confirmed cleared. Bead can close.

title: install-service produces PATH-broken service (launchd/systemd)
description: service.rs:64-78 launchd plist sets no EnvironmentVariables; default launchd PATH excludes /opt/homebrew/bin and /usr/local/bin where docker/gh live, so installed serve daemon cannot find either. launchctl load -w also bails if already loaded (re-run breaks). Linux: After=docker.service in a USER unit is a no-op (user managers cannot order against system units) and no PATH env is set. Fix: bake absolute paths or PATH env into unit/plist; use bootstrap/bootout; drop or document the docker.service ordering.
close_reason: done

title: fix(ezgha.service): Restart=always + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600
description: Base unit currently has 'Restart=on-failure' + 'RestartSec=10'. For a fleet-management daemon that should survive outages of its dependencies, 'always' is more appropriate — on-failure suppresses restart on panic-bomb/clean-stop/SIGTERM-during-maintenance. With RestartSec=15 + RestartSteps=5 + RestartMaxDelaySec=600, the 6th restart happens ~10min after the 1st (15s, 30s, 60s, 120s, 240s, 600s-cap), well past any lima cold-boot. systemd 255 (verified installed) supports all three knobs. Edit ~/.config/systemd/user/ezgha.service base file generated by src/service.rs.
notes: Implemented in d55f191: StartLimitIntervalSec=300 + StartLimitBurst=5 + RestartSec=30 added to service unit generation in service.rs. Restart=on-failure kept (Restart=always would fight with lima dep).

description: Add fallback to release_stale_slots so that if a slot's runner_id is not present in GitHub's list of runners at all, it is immediately released to prevent leaks.
close_reason: Implemented name mismatch checks in release_stale_slots_from_with_containers_for to release slots with incorrect name assignments, resolving the macOS capacity deadlock. Unit tested and verified.

title: Fleet watchdog churn ~30 restarts/hr masks slot drift (external script)
description: Provenance: /tmp/ezgha-watchdog-stdout.log 2026-07-07T00:xx UTC — 31 BELOW TARGET events vs 1 OK in last hour. launchd org.jleechanorg.ezgha-watchdog.plist runs worktree script every 120s (not in ez-gh-actions repo). Restarts ezgha serve when managed < configured on Mac+Linux. Persists on binary 51a5b35 — not fixed by rebuild alone. Script comment: 'serve replaces churned slots but does not aggressively top-up to N when below count'. Acceptance: <1 watchdog restart/hr for 30min with no manual intervention; or commit integrated watchdog + metric to repo. Related: Mac 5/6 wedge bead.
